test_description: Remove the note entirely and present the current explanation excerpt starting at “المعنى الإجمالي:”.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Whether the current pair collapses into an orphaned explanation without the note.
why_this_test_matters: This is the direct forced A/B/C question for the pair as given.

test_description: Compare the current generated summary note against a source-preserving context block containing the actual hadith material.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Whether the note is merely a weaker patch than preserved source context.
why_this_test_matters: The owner explicitly prefers keeping source material close to the excerpt when integrity is at stake.

test_description: Split explanation from explained text, then inject two similar hadith variants and check whether the system keeps the explanation tied to the right one.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Version/wording mismatch harm under blind separation.
why_this_test_matters: This is one of the owner’s central practical objections to clean separation-of-concerns.

test_description: Compare studying from the proof as found in the book alone versus the authoritative fetched proof alone versus both layers linked together.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Which integrity losses happen when one layer replaces the other.
why_this_test_matters: The owner explicitly wants both preserved and confirmed layers, not one collapsing the other.

test_description: Use a scholar explanation that presupposes one grading/authenticity assumption while the fetched proof layer defaults to another.
why_this_test_matters: A note that names the hadith broadly would not catch this deeper misalignment.

test_description: Test a scholar methodology where topic, proof, and explanation are deeply interwoven and cannot be cleanly separated by prewritten rules.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Failure of rigid separation under methodology variability.
why_this_test_matters: The owner explicitly fears unseen methodology patterns and wants intelligent diagnosis plus uncertainty gates.

test_description: For the current broken pair, compare default-visible note versus collapsible note.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Whether hiding the note worsens the current pair even if the ideal design would make notes secondary.
why_this_test_matters: The visibility answer for the current pair must not be confused with the ideal design answer.

test_description: Audit whether a fetched authoritative proof record is explicitly linked to the exact wording/version the scholar’s explanation appears to assume.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Silent drift between authoritative proof layer and explanation anchor.
why_this_test_matters: Without this audit, a clean architecture can still produce wrong scholarly pairings.

test_description: Make the note rich enough to feel readable while leaving the explained/explanation split untouched.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Whether readability can mask a still-corrupted structure.
why_this_test_matters: The owner explicitly does not want the note question to cap the deeper diagnosis.
